originalTitle: Police drop case against artist who depicted high-profile Australians in uniforms with Nazi symbols
summary: Michael Agzarian was charged despite internal legal advice that the images were ‘political satire’, court hears
content: Police charged an artist who depicted prominent Australians in military uniforms emblazoned with Nazi symbols despite internal legal advice the images were “political satire”, a court has heard. Police dropped the case against Michael Agzarian before the Downing Centre local court on Friday, almost a year after he had been charged with one count of displaying Nazi symbols by a public act without lawful excuse. Agzarian had displayed a poster on his shopfront window in Wagga Wagga ahead of last year’s federal election. The poster featured his local MP, Michael McCormack, the opposition leader, Peter Dutton, and mining magnates Gina Rinehart and Clive Palmer in the second world war German army uniforms, the court heard. The court had heard the artwork was adapted from an image from the US sitcom Hogan’s Heroes. McCormack, the Nationals MP for Riverina, made the complaint to police about the image. He told Guardian Australia after the charge against Agzarian was withdrawn that he made the report after several others in the community had also complained. Judge Karen Stafford, who oversaw a cost application and awarded Agzarian more than $12,000 in costs, outlined in court the legal advice given to police by their internal legal counsel. That advice said: “The crimes act does not define what a Nazi symbol is unlike the criminal code. But in our view none of the imagery would come within either the NSW or commonwealth legislation to be considered an offence. It is political satire.” Sign up for the Breaking News Australia email Stafford found the charges were laid with reasonable cause. But she criticised police for taking seven months to withdraw the charge, even after a hearing date was set. The charge carries a maximum penalty of 12 months in prison or an $11,000 fine. Documents tendered in court revealed Agzarian’s lawyer, Nick Hanna, wrote to police in September requesting they withdraw the charges because it was “inevitable that the prosecution will fail to establish that our client knew that the symbols that he was displaying were Nazi symbols”. However, that request was rejected. In February, police seized similar posters from Dissent cafe in Canberra. The artwork, by protest artist group Grow Up Art which is not connected to Agzarian, depicted various world leaders including the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, Russian president, Vladimir Putin, and US president, Donald Trump, in Nazi uniforms. The Canberra cafe owner David Howe, who had said the posters were artworks that expressed an anti-fascist message, was not charged. Hanna said outside court on Friday that his client should never have been charged. “Michael was dragged through this ruling prosecution for almost a year when he never should have been charged in the first place. “In Australia, we have a very longstanding tradition of political satire, and the idea that someone can be criminally charged and face imprisonment for that is really disturbing, and so this is a really important win for free speech.” On Friday, McCormack questioned the laws. “Police laid charges under the [2022] legislation in state law and quite frankly if it means people are allowed to do this to politicians then why even have the laws. “I’m all for free speech … but free speech comes with responsibility. “He dressed us up as Nazis, it wasn’t Hogan’s Heroes.” Agzarian said outside court the matter had taken a toll on him. “I’m an artist, and it was satire … The whole thing felt very authoritarian.”
chineseTitle: 警方撤销对艺术家的指控，该艺术家描绘了穿着带有纳粹标志的制服的知名澳大利亚人
chineseSummary: 法庭获悉，尽管内部法律建议这些图像是“政治讽刺”，但迈克尔·阿格扎里安仍被指控
chineseContent: 法庭获悉，警方指控一名艺术家描绘身穿印有纳粹标志的军装的澳大利亚知名人物，尽管内部法律建议这些图像是“政治讽刺”。周五，警方在唐宁中心地方法院撤销了对迈克尔·阿扎里安的指控，距离他被指控在无合法理由的情况下在公共场合展示纳粹标志的罪名已近一年。去年联邦选举之前，阿格扎里安在沃加沃加的店面橱窗上展示了一张海报。法庭获悉，海报上的主角是当地议员迈克尔·麦科马克、反对党领袖彼得·达顿以及矿业巨头吉娜·莱因哈特和克莱夫·帕尔默，他们身着二战时期的德国军装。法院获悉该艺术品改编自美国情景喜剧《霍根英雄》中的图像。里维纳的国民党议员麦科马克向警方投诉了这张照片。在对阿扎里安的指控被撤销后，他告诉《澳大利亚卫报》，他是在社区中其他几人也提出投诉后才做出这一报告的。负责监督费用申请并判给阿扎里安超过 12,000 美元费用的法官凯伦·斯塔福德 (Karen Stafford) 在法庭上概述了警方内部法律顾问向警方提供的法律建议。该建议称：“《犯罪法》没有定义纳粹标志与刑法不同的地方。但我们认为，新南威尔士州或联邦立法中没有任何图像会被视为犯罪。这是政治讽刺。”斯塔福德在注册澳大利亚突发新闻电子邮件后发现，这些指控是有合理理由的。但她批评警方即使在听证会日期确定后，仍花了七个月的时间才撤回指控。该指控最高可判处 12 个月监禁或 11,000 美元罚款。医生
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
